content.text:
Validating the diagram with Mermaid's parser to pinpoint the syntax error:

[REDACTED]

input.command:
cd /tmp && cat > issue198.mmd << 'EOF' sequenceDiagram participant Caller as Concierge caller
(discovery_tools / discovery_workflow) participant Runner as run_rebase_discovery_flow participant Seeder as seed_run_pack_assets
(shared helper) participant FS as Filesystem participant Flow as run_flow_async Caller->>Runner: source_repo: str Runner->>Runner: sanitize_source_repo -> repo: str Runner->>Runner: rebase_discovery_workflow_yaml_path -> yaml_path: Path Runner->>FS: mkdir run_root = workdir/rebase-flow-runs/ Runner->>FS: write run_root/workflow.rendered.yaml
{rendered_yaml: str} Runner->>Seeder: pack_root: Path, run_root: Path,
required=("schemas",), optional=("templates",) Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/schemas -> run_root/schemas
(required; abort on missing — discovery caller only) Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/templates -> run_root/templates
(optional; skipped if absent) Seeder-->>Runner: SeedResult{seeded: list[str], schema_files: list[str]} alt seeding failed Runner-->>Caller: {ok: false, error: str} else seeding ok Runner->>Flow: workflow_yaml: Path, cwd=run_root,
params={source_repo}, log_path: Path Flow-->>Runner: exit_code: int Runner-->>Caller: {ok: bool, exit_code: int, run_dir: str,
log_path: str, workflow_yaml: str,
source_repo: str, message: str} end EOF npx -y @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli@11 -i issue198.mmd -o issue198.svg 2>&1 || true

input.description: Test which mermaid constructs break parsing
Bisect mermaid parse failure line by line
Bisect mermaid parse with script file
Isolate semicolon vs uuid parse issues
Find all semicolons in mermaid diagram
Verify full diagram parses after semicolon fix

content.text:
The sequence diagram fails on GitHub because **Mermaid treats `;` in arrow labels as syntax**, not as plain text. ## Root cause On these two lines, the semicolon ends the message early and the rest of the line is invalid: ```text Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/schemas -> run_root/schemas
(required; abort on missing — discovery caller only) Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/templates -> run_root/templates
(optional; skipped if absent) ``` The parser reads everything after `;` as a new statement. That matches what Mermaid reports: a parse error around line 14, expecting an arrow but getting a newline. I confirmed with the same Mermaid version GitHub uses: the full diagram parses after removing or replacing those semicolons. ``, `
`, and `{ok: false, ...}` are not the problem here. ## What is fine These are OK in your diagram: - `
` in participant names (same pattern as other design docs in the repo) - `rebase-flow-runs/` - Curly braces and `list[str]` in labels ## How to fix Replace semicolons in **message text** with something else, for example: ```mermaid Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/schemas -> run_root/schemas
(required - abort on missing - discovery caller only) Seeder->>FS: copytree pack_root/templates -> run_root/templates
(optional - skipped if absent) ``` Or use the HTML entity `#59;` if you want a visible semicolon: ```mermaid (required#59; abort on missing) ``` Alternatively, shorten the labels and put the long explanation in prose below the diagram. ## Optional hardening For very dense labels (commas, parentheses, `=`, etc.), wrapping the whole label in quotes can help in some cases, but **semicolons must still be avoided or escaped** — quoting alone does not fix `;` in sequence-diagram messages. I can open a small PR on `architecture-design/issue-198` with that two-line fix if you want. [REDACTED]
